# Claude Collaborate

> Where Human Creativity Meets AI Intelligence — A unified sandbox for real-time human-AI collaboration

## Overview

Claude Collaborate is a unified sandbox environment for real-time human-AI collaboration. It brings together interactive workspaces, seamless communication, and creative tools in one interface — whiteboard, code editor, chess, and more.

## Quick Start

```bash
git clone https://github.com/mcp-tool-shop-org/claude-collaborate.git
cd claude-collaborate
pip install aiohttp
python server.py

# Open http://localhost:8877
```

## Environments

| Environment | Description |
|-------------|-------------|
| **Whiteboard** | Draw, sketch, and brainstorm visually |
| **Code Workshop** | HTML/CSS/JS editor with live preview |
| **Chess Workshop** | Strategy and tactics playground |
| **Capture Viewer** | Screenshots and recordings viewer |
| **GitHub Toolkit** | README and marketing generators |
| **Creative Lab** | Interactive experiments |
| **Voice Studio** | TTS integration (requires voice-soundboard) |
| **Template/Add Environment** | Starter for creating new environments |

## Architecture

```
claude-collaborate/
├── index.html           # Main UI with environment switcher
├── server.py            # aiohttp server
├── ws_bridge.py         # WebSocket bridge for Claude Code
├── whiteboard.html      # Drawing and brainstorming
├── code-playground.html # Live HTML/CSS/JS editor
├── chess.html           # Chess analysis board
├── capture-viewer.html  # Screenshot/recording viewer
├── github-toolkit.html  # README and marketing tools
└── adventures/          # Creative Lab experiments
```

## WebSocket Protocol

Real-time communication between browser and Claude Code.

### Client -> Server

| Type | Fields | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| user_message | content | Chat message from browser |
| typing_start | sender | Broadcast typing indicator to other clients |
| typing_stop | sender | End typing indicator |
| resume | session_id, last_seen_seq | Reconnect and replay missed messages |
| ping | (none) | Keepalive; server replies with pong |

### Server -> Client

| Type | Fields | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| connected | session_id, message | Welcome on connect |
| message_received | message_id, seq, session_id | Ack after user_message persisted |
| claude_response | content, message_id, seq | Full response from Claude Code |
| resume_complete | session_id, replayed | Reconnection confirmed |
| replay | (original message fields) | Missed message during reconnection |
| typing_start / typing_stop | sender, session_id | Ephemeral typing indicators |
| pong | (none) | Reply to ping |
| error | message | Human-readable error |

All sequenced messages carry a monotonic `seq` field and are buffered in a 500-entry ring for reconnection replay. Typing indicators are ephemeral (not sequenced or buffered).

### Reconnection Protocol

1. Client connects to `/ws` and receives `connected` with a `session_id`.
2. On disconnect, client reconnects and sends: `{ "type": "resume", "session_id": "<old>", "last_seen_seq": <N> }`.
3. Server replays all buffered messages with `seq > last_seen_seq` as `replay` type.
4. Server sends `resume_complete` with the count of replayed messages.
5. Clients should use exponential backoff for reconnection attempts.

### Streaming Protocol

Both `server.py` (via `POST /api/ws/stream`) and `ws_bridge.py` (via `post_stream`) support chunked streaming:

1. Claude Code sends chunks: `POST /api/ws/stream` with `{ "message_id": "<id>", "chunk": "text fragment", "done": false }`.
2. Server broadcasts each chunk as `stream_chunk` to all WebSocket clients with per-stream `seq`.
3. Final call with `"done": true` assembles the full response, persists it, broadcasts `stream_end`, and records in history.

## Server API (server.py — port 8877)

### GET /
Main UI with environment switcher.

### GET /{file}
Static files (whiteboard.html, chess.html, etc.).

### GET /ws
WebSocket bridge for real-time bidirectional communication.

### GET /api/ws/messages
Read pending user messages (destructive read — clears file after read).

### POST /api/ws/respond
Send response to browser. Broadcasts to all connected WebSocket clients.

### GET /api/ws/status
WebSocket bridge status (connected clients, version, timestamp).

### POST /api/ws/typing
Broadcast a typing indicator to all connected WebSocket clients.
Body: `{ "status": "start"|"stop", "sender": "claude" }`.

### POST /api/ws/stream
Stream a chunked response to WebSocket clients in real time.
Body: `{ "message_id": "<id>", "chunk": "text", "done": false }`.
When `done: true`, the full response is assembled, persisted, and a `stream_end` is broadcast.

### GET /api/metrics
Server metrics: uptime, total messages received, total responses sent, total WS connections, current clients, history buffer size.

### GET /api/sessions
Active WebSocket sessions with client count and last activity.

### GET /api/history?limit=N
Non-destructive read from the message history ring buffer. Default limit: 50.

### GET /health
Server health check.

### GET /adventures
List available adventures.

### GET /adventures/{filename}
Serve a specific adventure file.

## Standalone Bridge (ws_bridge.py)

The standalone WebSocket bridge runs independently on port **8878** (configurable via `--port`).
Use it when you only need the message relay without the full UI server on 8877.

| Endpoint | Method | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| `/ws` | WebSocket | Bidirectional message relay |
| `/api/messages` | GET | Read pending user messages |
| `/api/respond` | POST | Send a response to connected browsers |
| `/api/history` | GET | Non-destructive message history |
| `/health` | GET | Bridge health check |

## Creative Lab (adventures/)

The `adventures/` directory contains interactive experiment files served by the Creative Lab environment.
Adventures are standalone HTML pages loaded inside the Creative Lab iframe. List available adventures via `GET /adventures`.
